revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun]][[ja:井ノ内稲荷塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun, enkei.jpg|part_of={{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qid=Q123117022|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}}}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun (井ノ内稲荷塚古墳) |image = [[File:Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun, enkei.jpg|280px]]
Distant view (rounded rear mound on the left, square front mound on the right)
({{ill|Kyoto Nishiyama High School|ja|京都西山高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Nishiyama High School|1=Kyoto Nishiyama High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11375686}} Second Ground on the far left) |alternate_name = |所属 = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|en|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|qid=Q861246|lt=Nagaokakyo|1=Nagaokakyō, Kyoto}} Inouchi Konishi |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 56|coordinates = 28.37 |coordinates = 135|coordinates = 41|coordinates = 6.20 |omitted = JP-26 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |area = Mound length 46m
Height 4m (rear mound) |Burial Facility = Rear mound: {{ill|Single-sided corridor-style stone chamber|ja|片袖式横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Single-sided corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
Front mound: Wooden coffin direct burial |burial goods = Gilded bronze horse trappings, gold sword fittings, and numerous grave goods |陪塚 = |built = Early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} "Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun"
(Part of "Otokuni Kofun Cluster") |有形文化財 = excavations (Nagaokakyo City Designated designation1) |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |アイコン = Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE |ラベル = Inouchi
Inarizuka Kofun |ラベル位置 = right }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|34|56|30|N|135|41|0|E}} | zoom = 12 | float = right | width = 250| height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the National Historic site "Otokuni Kofun Cluster"}} | map_sizemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|34|56|59.71|N|135|41|42.78|E}} | map_dot_label1=Gotsukahara | map_dot_label-pos1=right | map_dot_label-color1=black | mark1=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE.png | mark-size1=15 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|34|56|42.20|N|135|41|50.53|E}} | map_dot_label2=Motoinari | map_dot_label-pos2=right | map_dot_label-color2=black | mark2=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SW.png | mark-size2=15 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|34|57|21.0|N|135|41|19.0|E}} | map_dot_label3=Terado Otsuka | map_dot_label-pos3=left | map_dot_label-color3=black | mark3=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE.png | mark-size3=15 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|34|55|52.40|N|135|40|31.28|E}} | map_dot_label4=Chōhōji Minamihara | map_dot_label-pos4=left | map_dot_label-color4=black | mark4=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SW.png | mark-size4=15 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|34|58|39.04|N|135|41|22.74|E}} | map_dot_label5=Tennō no Mori | map_dot_label-pos5=right | map_dot_label-color5=black | mark5=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE.png | mark-size5=15 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|34|54|42.5|N|135|40|39.2|E}} | map_dot_label6=Torimae | map_dot_label-pos6=right | map_dot_label-color6=black | mark6=Scallop-shaped tumulus-NNE.png | mark-size6=15 | mark-coord7= {{Coord|34|54|52.3|N|135|41|51.3|E}} | map_dot_label7=Egayama | map_dot_label-pos7=right | map_dot_label-color7=black | mark7=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE.png | mark-size7=20 | mark-coord8= {{Coord|34|57|27.50|N|135|41|39.85|E}} | map_dot_label8=Nanjō | map_dot_label-pos8=top | map_dot_label-color8=black | mark8=Round tumulus.png | mark-size8=10 | mark-coord9= {{Coord|34|56|40.75|N|135|40|46.05|E}} | map_dot_label9=Shiba | map_dot_label-pos9=left | map_dot_label-color9=black | mark9=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE.png | mark-size9=10 | mark-coord10= {{Coord|34|56|38.45|N|135|40|51.07|E}} | map_dot_label10=Inouchi Kurazuka | map_dot_label-pos10=top | map_dot_label-color10=black | mark10=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE.png | mark-size10=10 | mark-coord11= {{Coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.55|E}} | map_dot_label11=Mushikume Kurazuka | map_dot_label-pos11=right | map_dot_label-color11=black | mark11=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-NE.png | mark-size11=10 | mark-coord12= {{Coord|34|56|28.37|N|135|41|6.20|E}} | map_dot_label12=Inouchi Inarizuka | map_dot_label-pos12=bottom | map_dot_label-color12=black | mark12=Keyhole-shaped tumulus-SE.png | mark-size12=10 | mark-coord13= {{Coord|34|55|47.95|N|135|41|11.85|E}} | map_dot_label13=Imazato Otsuka | map_dot_label-pos13=right | map_dot_label-color13=black | mark13=Round tumulus.png | mark-size13=15 }} '''Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun''' (井ノ内稲荷塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|en|Nagaokakyō, Kyoto|qid=Q861246|lt=Nagaokakyo|1=Nagaokakyō, Kyoto}}, Kyoto Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja|乙訓古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokuni Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491395|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} and has been Historic site as a {{ill|National Historic Site of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the site have been Historic site as Nagaokakyo City Historic site. == Overview == The Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun is situated at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kyoto Basin|ja|京都盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Basin|1=Kyoto Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10883263}} on a low terrace extending from the Nishiyama mountain range (elevation 42 meters). The tumulus mound is well preserved, although the stone chamber was significantly damaged during the construction of {{ill|Nagaoka-kyō|en|Nagaoka-kyō|qid=Q1052803|lt=Nagaoka-kyō|1=Nagaoka-kyō}}. Currently, the mound is crowned by an Inari shrine. Archaeological excavations have been conducted since {{ill|1993|en|1993|qid=Q2065|lt=1993|1=1993}}. The tumulus has a keyhole type with its front section oriented southeast. Most of the mound was constructed with piled earth. No tiered construction, surface paving stones, or haniwa have been identified. The burial facilities consist of a single-sided {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear mound and a wooden coffin directly buried in the front mound. The rear stone chamber, measuring 10.1 meters in total length, is noted as an early-style Kinai region stone chamber. Excavations revealed numerous burial goods from both burial facilities, including gilt bronze horse trappings, gold sword fittings, personal ornaments, weapons, agricultural tools, horse gear, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. The kofun is estimated to date to the early {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}. Among the Otokuni Kofun Cluster, it is thought to have been constructed after the {{ill|Inouchi Kurazuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Kurazuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a high-Ranking burial site of the time. It holds significant importance as one of the earliest examples of corridor-style stone chambers along the right bank of the {{ill|Katsura River|en|Katsura River|qid=Q2909307|lt=Katsura River|1=Katsura River}}. However, the site reflects the traditional customs of the local elite by continuing the use of wooden coffins without nails, contrasting with the adoption of house-shaped Sarcophaguss at other contemporary sites like the {{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}}. In {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}, the tumulus was Historic site as part of the national historic site "Otokuni Kofun Cluster," and the burial goods were Historic site as municipal tangible cultural properties in {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}. == burial == The tumulus includes two burial facilities: a {{ill|single-sided corridor-style stone chamber|ja|片袖式横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/single-sided corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear mound and a wooden coffin burial in the front mound. === Rear Mound Stone Chamber === The stone chamber in the rear mound measures 10.1 meters in total length. The chamber’s main room measures 4.6 meters in length and 2.2 meters in width, while the passageway is 5.5 meters long and 1.3 meters wide. The upper section of the chamber was damaged in later periods when stones were removed. The walls use large stone slabs, particularly at the chamber’s entrance, where stones over 1.5 meters wide and 1 meter high are placed. The floor features gravel, and a wooden partition might have been used as a closure structure. burial goods found in the chamber suggest two burials were placed parallel to the main axis. A traditional wooden coffin was discovered within the gravel flooring, measuring approximately 1.7–1.8 meters in length and 0.4–0.5 meters in width. === Front Mound Wooden Coffin === The wooden coffin was directly buried at the mound’s summit, oriented perpendicular to the tumulus axis. The coffin is 3.4 meters long and constructed without nails. It is surrounded by a burial pit measuring 5 meters east-west and 3 meters north-south. == burial goods == burial goods discovered include: * Gilt bronze horse trappings * Gold sword fittings * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} == designation1 designation1s == * National Historic site: "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" (since {{ill|2016|en|2016|qid=Q25245|lt=2016|1=2016}}) * Municipal designation1: excavations (Historic site in {{ill|2007|en|2007|qid=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}}) == See Also == * {{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Treasures Database|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}} * [https://www.city.nagaokakyo.lg.jp/0000005668.html 乙訓古墳群 > 井ノ内稲荷塚古墳] - Nagaokakyo City Website * [https://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/inari.html 井ノ内稲荷塚古墳第5次調査] - Osaka University {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|version=99996042|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:井ノ内稲荷塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q116257052}} [[Category:History of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Otokuni Kofun Group]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
